name: Austrian Gugelhupf
recipeInstructions:
1. Place warm milk in a medium mixing bowl. Sprinkle yeast over the milk.
2. Add 1/2 cup flour. Beat with a large spoon for about 1 minute.
3. Cover and let rise in a warm place until almost double (approximately 40 minutes).
4. In a large mixer bowl, beat butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
5. Add eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
6. Stir in the yeast mixture, raisins, currants, orange peel, and salt.
7. Gradually stir in the remaining 3-1/2 cups flour.
8. Grease and flour a 9-cup gugelhupf mold or 12-cup fluted tube pan.
9. Arrange whole almonds in a design in the bottom of the pan.
10. Carefully spoon batter over almonds.
11. Cover and let rise in a warm place until almost double (about 1 hour).
12. Bake in a 350°F oven for about 40 minutes.
13. If necessary, cover the top with foil during the last 15 minutes of baking to prevent over-browning.
14. Remove from pan and cool on a wire rack.
15. Before serving, sift powdered sugar over the cake.
